thesis: Debates over the role of the federal government contributed to a large extent to the growth of political parties from 1800 to 1854 because fights over national power repeatedly sorted Americans into opposing camps.
contextualization: Before 1800 the United States was still recovering from the weak Articles of Confederation period.
